{{Quote|The famed Jedi Guardian.|[[Canderous Ordo]]|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
'''Kavar''' was a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and member of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and [[Jedi Civil War]]. He was also close friends with [[Meetra Surik]], who learned under him as an informal [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] prior to the Mandalorian Wars.

==Biography==
{{Quote|The Force works in mysterious ways, it seems. There are times I'm not convinced it doesn't have a sense of humor.|Kavar to Meetra Surik|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
Little is known about Kavar's early life. It is possible that he fought in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. It is also known that, at some point, he informally trained Meetra Surik who would eventually become known as the Jedi Exile. He would become good friends with her at some point. At the beginning of the Mandalorian Wars, Kavar was involved in skirmishes against the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]. The Mandalorians came to believe that Kavar, the "famed [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]]," would be placed in command of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] overall war effort once the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] entered the war.<ref name="KotOR II" />

However, the Council chose to remain neutral throughout the conflict, and it was [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]] who led a force of rebellious Jedi in their place. Many among the Mandalorian survivors often wondered how differently the war would have gone had Master Kavar, not Revan, been leading the Jedi during those fateful years.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref> After the war with the Mandalorians was over, the High Council [[Trial of Meetra Surik|judged]] Surik for joining Revan in his crusade against the Mandalorians. The Council chose to banish her, but not because she followed Revan. They sensed that she was a [[Wound in the Force/Legends|wound in the Force]], and were afraid of what might happen if she remained in the Order.

Little information is known about Kavar during the [[Jedi Civil War]] except that he fought alongside the Jedi ranks against the forces of Darth Malak. He was presumed dead at some point during these events, but that was far from the truth.<ref name="KotOR II" />

===Exile on Onderon===
{{Quote|I imagine that you hold little love for anyone on the Jedi Council anymore, even an old friend.|Kavar to Meetra Surik|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
After the Jedi Civil War, Kavar went to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] and became the advisor of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Talia (Onderon)|Talia]]. He would stay in the shadows of the Royal Palace, but when he did speak, he was described as being "cryptic as hell." As tensions rose between Queen Talia and her cousin, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vaklu]], Kavar urged Talia to wait until the [[Darth Nihilus|true threat]], Vaklu's unseen supporters, showed itself.<ref name="KotOR II" />

When the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' attempted to land in [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]], Vaklu's forces under [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Tobin (Human)|Tobin's]] command fired upon it, sparking a [[Blockade of Onderon|dogfight]] between Onderonian forces and the Republic and creating more tension in the city. Through the alliance of the Mandalorians on [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''Ebon Hawk'', Meetra Surik, made arrangements through a doctor named [[Dhagon Ghent]] to meet Kavar. Kavar met with Surik in the [[Unidentified Iziz cantina|Iziz Cantina]]. Just as Surik was to learn the full circumstances behind her trial, Tobin's forces showed up and opened fire in the cantina. Kavar [[Force Stasis/Legends|stunned]] Tobin's men and escaped to the palace, while Surik was forced to fight her way through several troops back to her ship. As he ran, Kavar told Surik that he would get word to her when he was able.<ref name="KotOR II" />

[[File:Kavar_fight.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Kavar with his two lightsabers.]]
Not too long after these events, Kavar sent a message to the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Kelborn]], telling him to contact Surik and tell her that he had arranged safe passage for her back to Onderon. But Surik got the message too late: the [[Council of Lords]], under the influence of General Vaklu, decreed that Talia was guilty of [[Treason/Legends|treason]] soon after the message was sent. With the Onderonian military divided between support for Talia and Vaklu, the [[Onderon Civil War]] began and passage to the planet was no longer safe. When Surik heard the message, she rushed back to Dxun and enlisted the aid of the Mandalorians there. She then used a [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]] to reach Onderon.<ref name="KotOR II" />

While Surik made her way to the palace, Kavar and a group of troops fought at the palace gates against Vaklu's supporters, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and their beasts. Kavar was forced to retreat inside the palace, where he coordinated the battle. The Exile eventually reached Kavar, and he directed her to Captain [[Kadron]]. When Surik made it to the throne room, Kavar rushed in and eliminated some of Vaklu's troops. He stayed back, however, when Talia and Surik fought Vaklu themselves. He would also remain quiet when Talia decreed that Vaklu was to be executed, though Vaklu was ultimately spared and arrested at the insistance of Surik. The short but devastating civil war was ended, with Onderon's rightful ruler still on the throne.<ref name="KotOR II" />

Kavar answered Surik's questions, but was shocked to learn that [[Atris]] survived the attack on Katarr, not knowing that she had never attended the [[Conclave at Katarr|conclave]]. He agreed to join the Council on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] after the Exile had gathered all of the remaining Jedi Masters.<ref name="KotOR II" />

===Death===
[[File:RevanForceDrain.jpg|thumb|right|Kavar is killed by [[Kreia]]'s [[Force drain/Legends|Force drain]] along with [[Vrook Lamar]] and [[Zez-Kai Ell]].]]
Once Surik had done so, he met with fellow Masters [[Vrook Lamar]] and [[Zez-Kai Ell]] at the rebuilt [[Jedi Enclave]], and encountered Surik for what would be the last time. The Council decided that it was time Surik knew the truth, and told her everything: she was a wound in the Force, she had cut herself off from the Force. All three of them found it necessary to rid Surik of the Force, fearing she was a threat to life itself. However, as they were about to cut Surik's ties to the Force, [[Kreia]], whom Kavar and the other masters had previously believed to be dead, entered.<ref name="KotOR II" />

After presenting herself, Kreia decided to make the three Jedi "see the Force through the eyes of the Exile" and killed them with an extreme version of [[Force drain/Legends|Force drain]] leaving them dead and drained of the Force.<ref name="KotOR II" />

==Powers and abilities==
Kavar practiced [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]] variants of the [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] forms [[Form IV/Legends|Ataru]], [[Form V/Legends|Shien]], [[Form VI/Legends|Niman]], and [[Form VII/Legends|Juyo]]. He fought using two blue lightsabers—one regular lightsaber in his right hand, and a [[Shoto lightsaber/Legends|shoto]] in his left. In his first meeting with Surik on Onderon, he was carrying only his regular saber with him. He could also use sever Force ability which he used on Surik.

Kavar was a master of the advanced [[Force form]]s, and was so proficient in using [[Force Stasis/Legends|Force Stun]] that he could stun multiple targets simultaneously. He was capable of using [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force wave]] against enemies, and also used the [[Force healing/Legends|Jedi healing]] ability.<ref name="KotOR II" />

==Behind the scenes==
From conversations between Kavar and Meetra Surik, it is known that she received some degree of training under the Jedi Master; however, Kavar specifically states that she was '''not''' his Padawan although he stated that he considered making her his Padawan.<ref name="KotOR II" />

He was voiced by [[Tom Kane]].

===Alternate endings===
If the player has set the Jedi Exile to the dark side, they will side with General Vaklu. The Exile will then battle against Kavar, who will be surprised to see his former friend fallen so far. As a result, the player is forced to kill Kavar, resulting in his absence on Dantooine.

Should the Exile support Talia, there is still a chance to kill Kavar in the Enclave if the Exile killed either of the other Masters, Vrook or Zez-Kai Ell.

In cut content of the game, Kreia has more dialogue for the Masters after she drains them of the Force. They do not die instantly; instead, Kreia [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Crush|Force Crushes]] or [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force choke|Force Chokes]] them. Kreia will ask Kavar if, during his stay on Onderon, he had felt [[Battle of Dxun|what had taken place]] on its moon [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]].
